summary: Iteration 5 — branch protection, rollback, and SitePreview/health probe verified end-to-end. Backend: full pytest suite 50/50 PASS (added TestAppsIter5 with 8 new tests covering PATCH tier+protected_branches, redeploy 403/200 branch gating, rollback creates a new building deployment with same branch+commit and 'Rollback to ...' message, rollback 400 for in-flight, rollback 403 under branch protection, GET /apps/{id}/health for live URL + no_url path, and a regression check that recent deployments are not stuck in 'building' indefinitely). Frontend Playwright (demo@deployhub.dev, opened novabrew-web-renamed-x-t): SitePreview present (3 colored chrome dots, iframe, all 4 stat tiles — Status, Response, Uptime, Avg). Reload button on SitePreview fires GET /api/apps/{id}/health (1 call captured) and forces iframe key change. Settings → Branch protection panel shows tier dropdown + Allowed branches CSV input; input toggles disabled correctly when tier=development; Save changes triggers 'Settings saved' toast. With tier=production saved, Deploy modal renders the protection banner ('PRODUCTION TIER · only branches [main] can be deployed') — data-testid='deploy-protection-banner' confirmed. Deployments tab shows 13 rollback buttons (data-testid='rollback-{id}') across finished rows; clicking one accepts the confirm dialog, fires POST /api/apps/{id}/rollback/{dep_id}, and shows 'Rollback queued' toast. tier=development restored at the end of the run.

issues: No '// current' marker rendered for the in-flight or most-recent live deploy because all visible deployments on the demo first app are in a 'failed' state — every row therefore shows a rollback button (13 of them). Functionally correct, but if a 'live' or 'building' row exists later the spec expects '// current' to render in its actions cell. Worth a quick visual confirmation once the demo container goes healthy.

critical_code_review_comments: routers/apps.py — branch-protection check is correctly applied in BOTH redeploy() and rollback() (rollback delegates to redeploy via RedeployIn, but also pre-checks so the user gets a 403 even before the redeploy path runs). Default allowed=['main'] when production tier with no list saved is a sensible fallback.
rollback() correctly rejects in-progress deployments (400) and crafts commit_message='Rollback to <sha7>' when commit is known else 'previous deploy'.
app_health() handles framing detection by parsing X-Frame-Options and CSP frame-ancestors — covers the iframe sandbox UX nicely. Title extraction via regex is bounded to 200 chars (good).
SitePreview.jsx mounts iframe with sandbox + key={reloadKey} so the reload button fully remounts the iframe. Matches Option A.

DeployModal.jsx — `branchBlocked = isProduction && branch && !allowed.includes(branch)` correctly disables the submit button and swaps the label to 'Branch protected'. Banner also renders independent of branch selection.
AppDetail.jsx is now ~500 lines — close to but still under the 700 line guideline. Continued growth (e.g., adding monitoring chart) might justify splitting Settings panel into its own file.

data-testid coverage for new features is excellent: site-preview, deploy-protection-banner, settings-tier-select, settings-protected-input, rollback-{id}, settings-save-btn, tab-{name} all present and stable.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (SitePreview, health-reload, branch-protection settings + deploy modal banner, rollback button + POST + toast all verified)

seed_data_creation: During testing the demo first app (novabrew-web-renamed-x-t) cycled through tier=production several times via PATCH and one rollback was fired (creates one extra deployment row). Final state: tier=development, protected_branches preserved as last set ['main']; protection is inert when tier=development. This is the same idempotent footprint as iter4.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter5 features (branch protection, rollback, health probe + SitePreview) are fully green. Backend regression suite is at 50/50. New test class TestAppsIter5 in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers all three. Note: every visible deployment on the seeded first app currently has status='failed' (Coolify-hosted demo container is unhealthy), so the 'most-recent live → // current' marker is not exercisable from the seeded data alone. If you want to verify the '// current' label, first wait for or trigger a successful deploy. The demo first app id is now 8227672f-1c09-4864-a7f8-51a4674bd3d0 (workspace Acme Studio).
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found in this iteration.
